subject: Civics
overall_learning_objectives: Analyse the concept of marginalisation and its causes
Examine the social and economic conditions of marginalised communities
Understand the lives and challenges of Adivasi communities
Evaluate the issues faced by minority groups in society
Analyse the role of stereotypes and prejudice in marginalisation
Understand how equality and inclusion can reduce marginalisation

lesson_plan.title: Concept of Marginalisation
student_activities: Analyse situations where certain groups lack opportunities
Write a short explanation of how marginalisation affects people

lesson_plan.title: Adivasis and Their Social Conditions
topics_covered: Distribution of Adivasi communities in India
student_activities: Identify regions where Adivasi communities live
Discuss the importance of cultural identity

lesson_plan.title: Adivasis, Forests and Development
student_activities: Analyse how development projects affect Adivasi communities
Discuss possible solutions to reduce displacement

lesson_plan.title: Minorities and Marginalisation
topics_covered: Social and economic challenges faced by minority groups
Findings of reports studying minority conditions
student_activities: Discuss how minority groups may face discrimination
Write examples of challenges faced by minorities

lesson_plan.title: Stereotypes, Prejudice and Discrimination
student_activities: Identify examples of stereotypes in society
Explain how prejudice can lead to discrimination

lesson_plan.title: Equality, Inclusion and Chapter Review
student_activities: Write a short reflection on the importance of equality

teacher_tip: Encourage students to critically analyse how stereotypes and unequal access to resources can affect communities and discuss ways society can promote equality and inclusion.
